45.reason: [loop condition uses j > i but accesses a[j] where j can be equal to cnt, which is out of bounds since valid indices are 0 to cnt-1]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [loop condition uses j > i but accesses a[j] where j can be equal to cnt, which is out of bounds since valid indices are 0 to cnt-1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition uses j > i but accesses a[j], which may go out of bounds since valid indices are 0 to cnt-1. Should be j >= i+1 or j = cnt - 1 down to i+1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition should be j >= i+1 or similar; using j = cnt as starting index accesses out-of-bounds array element a[cnt], which is invalid since valid indices are 0 to cnt-1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition uses j > i but accesses a[j], which may go out of bounds since valid indices are 0 to cnt-1. Should be j >= i+1 or j from i+1 to cnt-1. Also, this nested loop logic for merging duplicates is flawed and inefficient.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition accesses a[j] with j=cnt, which is out of bounds (valid indices 0 to cnt-1)]

However, the main issue is that the program doesn't handle file open failure. If fopen fails, p is NULL and fgets causes undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [If fopen failed (p is NULL), fclose(p) is undefined behavior. Should only close if p != NULL.]

14.reason: [If the file "article.txt" does not exist, fopen returns NULL and subsequent operations on p will cause undefined behavior. The program should check if p is NULL and handle the error appropriately.]
